articles.title: Odisha extends summer holidays in Puri; Khordha shifts to morning classes amid heatwave conditions - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
As Odisha continues to grapple with intense heatwave conditions and high humidity, district administrations have announced fresh measures to protect schoolchildren from extreme weather. While school holidays in Puri have been extended till June 22, authorities in Khordha have revised school timings and directed institutions to function during cooler morning hours.
According to the official notification, school holidays in Puri, which were earlier scheduled to continue till June 20, have now been extended till June 22 due to the prevailing heatwave.
The decision has been taken as a precautionary measure to safeguard students from rising temperatures and uncomfortable weather conditions.

Meanwhile, the District Project Office of Samagra Shiksha, Khordha, has issued an order directing all Government, Government-aided and private-managed schools to conduct morning classes from June 19 to June 25. The revised schedule will apply to students from Sishu Vatika to Class XII.
As per the order, classes will be held from 6:30 AM to 10:30 AM. School authorities have been instructed to make adequate arrangements for safe drinking water, ORS, first-aid facilities, proper ventilation, and the overall safety and security of students.
The administration has also clarified that Mid-Day Meal and PM POSHAN activities will continue as usual within the available school hours. Headmasters, teachers, and staff members have been asked to remain vigilant and take all necessary precautions in view of the weather conditions.
Officials stated that the measures are aimed at ensuring uninterrupted academic activities while prioritising the health and well-being of students. The order further warns that any violation of the instructions will be viewed seriously, and action will be taken as per the rules.
